LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MOLLY PETTIT'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S UNTIMELY ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND DEFENDANT'S RATIONAL FOR THE DELAY IS UNTENABLE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16(C)) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE MOTION WAS FILED FOR DELAY OR, IN FACT, HAS CAUSED ANY DELAY GIVEN THE ON-GOING DISCOVERY, AND THE COURT HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION SO AS TO BE ABLE TO ADDRESS WHETHER THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WAS "FRIVOLOUS". ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCES WERE MADE BY THE PARTIES. TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. DEFENDANT ANITA MARGRILL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: PLAINTIFF ALLEGES CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION IN PARAGRAPH ELEVEN; REQUEST FOR TREBLE DAMAGES IS BASED ON 37.9(F) FOR CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION. UNCERTAINTY IS DISFAVORED AND THE COMPLAINT IS SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR AS TO WHAT RELIEF PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING FOR WHAT FACTUAL CLAIM. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: RULING BASED ON FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION BEING OVERRULED. SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION: THE FACTS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH ELEVEN REFLECT A CLAIM OF BAD FAITH. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER FOR JUDGE PATRICK MAHONEY'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MOLLY PETTIT'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S UNTIMELY ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS.
APPEARANCES: JOEL LIBERSON, ESQUIRE (LIBERSON & WOLFORD, 415.677.4110) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. STEVEN S. ABERN, ESQUIRE (HAAPALA ALTURA THOMPSON & ABERN, 510.763.2324) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND DEFENDANT'S RATIONAL FOR THE DELAY IS UNTENABLE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16(C)) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE MOTION WAS FILED FOR DELAY OR, IN FACT, HAS CAUSED ANY DELAY GIVEN THE ON-GOING DISCOVERY, AND THE COURT HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION SO AS TO BE ABLE TO ADDRESS WHETHER THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WAS "FRIVOLOUS". ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2007 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearances were made by the parties. Tentative ruling was adopted. DEFENDANT ANITA MARGRILL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: PLAINTIFF ALLEGES CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION IN PARAGRAPH ELEVEN; REQUEST FOR TREBLE DAMAGES IS BASED ON 37.9(F) FOR CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION. UNCERTAINTY IS DISFAVORED AND THE COMPLAINT IS SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR AS TO WHAT RELIEF PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING FOR WHAT FACTUAL CLAIM. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: RULING BASED ON FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION BEING OVERRULED. SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION: THE FACTS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH ELEVEN REFLECT A CLAIM OF BAD FAITH. Prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order for Judge Patrick Mahoney's signature. (302/PJB/AP) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
